message:
fix(e2e/bench): create report parent directories before writing HTML/JSON output

write_html_report and write_json_report previously called
File::create / std::fs::write directly on the user-supplied output
path. If any parent directory in the path didn't already exist (the
common case when CI passes something like target/bench-reports/<run>/
report.json without pre-creating the directory) the write failed with
ENOENT and the bench reported a generic "Could not create HTML file"
or "Could not write JSON report" line, then exited 0 having silently
discarded the artifact.

ensure_report_parent_dir(path, report_kind) does std::fs::create_dir_all
for the parent component if the parent is non-empty (paths with no
parent component, e.g. "bench.json", are accepted as-is). On failure
it emits a labelled error mentioning which report kind and which
parent directory failed, matching the existing eprintln style.

Both write_html_report and write_json_report now call it before
opening the file. The check is short-circuit-on-failure: if the parent
can't be created, we return without attempting the write rather than
falling through to a confusing File::create ENOENT.

Test write_json_report_creates_parent_directories exercises the JSON
path against a tempdir-anchored "<temp>/nested/bench.json" target
where "nested/" does not exist; the test asserts the file was written
and contains the schema-version sentinel, proving the parent dir was
created end-to-end.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
